Malware Recruitment Scam Alert - @vadeusnetwork   I want to expose a dangerous tactic being used in the crypto/Web3 hiring space.  I was contacted by the verified X account “@VadeusNetwork” for a moderator/community role. At first, everything looked legitimate:  - Verified profile - Professional branding - Community role offer - Standard hiring-style conversation  Then they sent me a “DocSend” document link: docsend(dot)com/view/d8t573pcqr6dia72  At first glance, it looks normal. But when opened, the page says: “This document requires the DocSend application for secure viewing.”  Then it pushes users to download an app from: docsendapplication.online  That domain is NOT official DocSend or Dropbox infrastructure.  Real DocSend uses: docsend.com  NOT: docsendapplication.online  This is a classic malware/social engineering flow:  - Use a trusted platform name (DocSend) - Create urgency/professionalism through a fake hiring process - Push a fake “secure viewer app” - Trick victims into installing malware  This type of attack specifically targets: Web3 workers, Moderators, Community managers, partnership managers, freelancers, startup contributors  because we constantly open decks, proposals, and onboarding docs. I did not execute the file outside the VM environment, but less technical users could easily fall for this.  Be careful: - Never install “viewer apps” from random hiring links - Verify domains carefully - Use sandbox environments - Assume crypto recruitment DMs are high-risk until proven otherwise  If anyone else received similar messages from this project/account, reply below.  Stay safe & Fk you scammers @vadeusnetwork

Fast16 was a "cyberweapon", tool designed to corrupt uranium-compression simulations central to Iran's nuclear weapon design. the targets as LS-DYNA and AUTODYN, two finite element solvers. The malware fast16 used a kernel-level filesystem filter driver to patch executable code on-the-fly without touching the files on disk. It only attacked Intel-Fortran-compiled binaries, and only during specific simulation phases - full-scale transient blast runs. The payload required three conditions to act: (1) an explosives-specific Equation of State, (2) a variable reaching five times its initial value, and (3) material density crossing 30 g/cm³ - the threshold uranium only reaches under implosion-type nuclear weapon compression. Once triggered, it silently degraded stress tensor outputs (pressure, compressibility) to 1-42% of their true values, scaled gradually to avoid obvious artifacts. The message to the engineers was: your bomb doesn't work. Alternatively: your bomb works better than it actually does. Someone doing this understood nuclear weapons physics well enough to know which output values, if subtly wrong, would silently wreck the design process.  That was not Google-searchable knowledge. security.com/threat-intellig…

LLM 애플리케이션을 위한 OWASP Top 10 2025 의 10가지 항목을 모두 공부하였습니다.   OWASP — Open Worldwide Application Security Project   OWASP은 비영리 보안 단체입니다. 전 세계 보안 전문가들이 모여서 "가장 위험한 취약점 Top 10"을 주기적으로 발표합니다. AI가 급속도로 퍼지면서 2023년부터 LLM 버전을 별도로 만들었습니다.  OWASP LLM Top 10은 전 세계 보안 전문가들이 실제 공격 사례를 분석해서 "이게 지금 가장 위험하다"고 합의한 목록입니다.   LLM을 활용한 에이전트를 직접 만들어본 상태에서, 그 시스템이 어떻게 공격당할 수 있는지, 어떻게 방어해야 하는지를 업계 표준 기준으로 전부 점검해보고 공부했습니다.  물론 제가 알지못하는 어려운 용어와 개념들은 llm에게 물어가며 함께 공부했습니다. 지금 제 수준에서는 절반정도 느낌만 이해하는 수준이지만, 이후의 커리큘럼을 진행해 나가는데 감을 잡는 느낌으로 공부했습니다.
"AI 모델 하나의 판단을 어디까지 믿을까?"  같은 모델이 자기 결과를 검증하면 맹점 공유. 다른 모델끼리 교차로 보면, 코드 보안에서 단일 모델이 놓친 취약점 Union이 나옵니다.  오픈소스(brotli/snappy/jansson 등) 메인테이너 보고 → 일부 PR merge.  #ClaudeCode #LLM #AISafety

요즘 AI 에이전트 뉴스에서 봐야 할 포인트는 “모델이 더 똑똑해졌다”가 아니라 “이걸 어디까지 믿고 맡길 수 있냐”임.  OpenAI는 Codex를 설명하면서 샌드박스, 승인 지점, 네트워크 정책, 로그를 전면에 세웠고, Microsoft는 100개+ 전문 에이전트를 묶은 보안 시스템으로 Windows 취약점 16개를 찾았다고 공개함.  근데 동시에 Microsoft Research는 에이전트가 일정 조율이나 가격 협상 같은 상황에서 사용자의 이익을 충분히 지키지 못한다고 지적함.  즉 에이전트 도입의 핵심 질문은 “무슨 모델 쓰냐”가 아니라:  이 에이전트가 어떤 권한을 갖는가 어디까지 자동 실행하는가 언제 사람 승인을 받는가 실패했을 때 어떤 로그가 남는가 상대와 협상할 때 정말 우리 편인가  앞으로 기업의 AI 경쟁력은 프롬프트 실력이 아니라 권한 설계, 감사 로그, 평가 체계, 샌드박스 운영에서 갈릴 가능성이 큼.  에이전트는 채팅창이 아니라 운영체계로 봐야 함.  openai.com/index/running-cod… microsoft.com/en-us/research…

🚨 통신보안?  사이버 전쟁터에서 살아남을 보안 대장주를 알아보자  인공지능이 취약점을 다 찾아내는 무서운 시대가 왔다. 앤스로픽 기술이 수만 개의 보안 구멍을 다 찾아냈다. 이런 혼란 속에서 진짜 보안 기업들이 돈을 벌고 있다  대장주 기업은 덩치가 제일 큰데도 성장 속도가 정말 빠르다.  대형 은행들이 믿고 쓰지만 최근 주가는 좀 느렸다.  크라우드스트라잌 $CRWD 은 클라우드 보안의 일인자고 AI 기술이 아주 훌륭하다. 주가도 많이 올랐지만 그만큼 사람들 기대가 하늘을 찌른다.   포티넷 $FTNT 는 방화벽 성능이 좋고 정부 기관도 애용한다. 성장세는 조금 느려도 수익은 꾸준히 내는 탄탄한 기업이다.  클라우드플레어 $NET 은 웹사이트 공격 막아주는 기술로 전 세계를 잡고 있다. 매출이 매년 폭발하지만 주가 변동성은 항상 조심해야 한다.  지스캐일러 $ZS 는 제로 트러스트 기술로 원격 근무 시대를 장악했다. 고점 대비 주가는 많이 내렸지만 사업은 여전히 잘 굴러간다.   옥타 $OKTA 는 로그인 관리를 아주 편하게 만들어주는 강자다. 회사를 많이 합병해서 덩치를 키웠는데 관리가 관건이다.  쳌포인트소프트웨어 $CHKP 는 가격이 아주 싸고 실속 있는 보안 기술을 가졌다. 성장은 조금 답답해도 안정감 하나는 확실히 보여준다.  보안 시장은 앞으로 더 커질 것이고 경쟁은 더 치열해진다.  매수/매도 추천아님(Not financial advice)

As soon as smoke was noticed, railway staff and emergency teams responded quickly, ensuring that passengers from the affected coach were evacuated safely. Initial reports confirmed that no injuries or casualties were reported.  Fire brigade personnel and railway officials immediately reached the location to bring the situation under control. Preliminary information suggested that the blaze may have been caused by a short circuit, although authorities have launched a detailed investigation to determine the exact reason behind the incident. Train movement on the busy Delhi-Mumbai route was also briefly affected during the operation.  Railway officials assured passengers that safety remained the top priority and necessary arrangements were being made to assist travelers. The timely coordination between railway staff and rescue teams played a crucial role in controlling the fire quickly, while the incident has renewed discussions around regular safety inspections and maintenance in premium train services like the Rajdhani Express.

The aerodrome cabal is probably the most toxic and net negative force in this space   On one side you have efforts like DeFi United, where the whole space rallied to scramble together coverage for Aave's bad debt after the Kelp incident. A collective response to a collective problem.  On the other side you have Fluid, which made the honorable decision (with zero obligation to do so) to cover the ~$8m in bad debt that USDC and USDT depositors were exposed to from the Resolv incident. Alone. No external help. Putting users first.   And what does aerodrome do? Instead of building anything resembling a useful product beyond their ponzinomics, they spend their time and energy trying to insinuate wrongdoing and cast doubt on Fluid's solvency (knowing very well that for any lending protocol, FUD around solvency is the most dangerous thing).  From my read, and that is only what is public and what i can see onchain is that Fluid decided to cover the bad debt with their business treasury. Structurally identical to Aave using its treasury to cover the Kelp hack, with the one difference that Fluid is socering all the bad debt alone.  To execute, they replaced the bad debt with an unsecured credit line originally approved for another purpose. This did not worsen the position of any USDC or USDT depositor. It transitioned the system from a state of bad debt to a state of solvency backed by an unsecured loan against Fluid itself.  To suggest USDC and USDT depositors were put at harm through this step is braind dead. They transitioned from a state of bad debt of 8 m to being creditors to an 8m credit line to Fluid, which in the worst case (Fluid not paying it down) would place them in the original situation to begin with, a situation of 8m in bad debt.  Obviously Fluid only did this because it is clear from their on chain treasury and the strength of their business that they can and will pay down that credit this week.  Was it necessary to do it this way?   Yes it seems clear they had to settle with Resolv quickly, and unwinding treasury positions in that timeframe wasn't feasible.  Should they have communicated the intermediate step better?  Probably, to prevent any confusion.  In any case, instead of celebrating Fluid for choosing to cover its users (something vault curators on Morpho have not done) the aerodrome cabal desperately tries to spin it into a scandal.  From where I sit, Fluid has consistently put its users first. This was the first instance of bad debt on Fluid lending market, but they also covered all losses in the Lite ETH vault that came from market conditions creating negative carry. Again, no obligation. Spiking ETH borrow rates are a known risk every staked ETH looper accepts in exchange for the elevated yield in normal regimes.  Two different cultures. One builds, absorbs losses, protects depositors. The other tweets.

Threat Intelligence Alert: Investigation into cczauvr.bh As part of an in-depth open-source threat intelligence operation, our team has analyzed the domain cczauvr.bh. This is a recent mirror (registered ~1 May 2026) of the long-running underground “PRIVATE CC SHOP” known as CCZAUVR, specializing in the sale of stolen credit cards, fullz, dumps, and related carding services. High-risk cybercrime infrastructure. #CyberThreatIntel #Carding Thread 2/6 Key technical findings: • Protected by Ghost DDoS Protection + FastFlux + Cloudflare proxying. • Displays only a waiting queue page (EN/RU) with instructions not to refresh. • WHOIS fully redacted; registered via DotWee Limited (Hong Kong) with privacy proxy in Mahe, Seychelles. • Multiple active mirrors identified: cczauvr.cc, cczauvr.sale, cczauvr.vip, cczauvr.shop, cczauvr.live and variants. All share identical infrastructure. Thread 3/6 Underground activity: The operator, using the alias “cczauvr”, has maintained a thread on the carding forum crdpro.at since January 2025 (now 63+ pages). The shop claims operations since 2008 and advertises an inventory exceeding 20 million CCs. Services include BIN search, checkers, and fullz. Payments accepted exclusively in Bitcoin and Litecoin via temporary wallets. Thread 4/6 Communication channels: • Official support via internal site tickets only. • Associated Telegram bot: @cczauvrofficial_bot • Updates channel: @cczauvr_official_updates Community feedback remains mixed: large stock but frequent complaints regarding dead cards, failed checkers, non-credited deposits, and slow refunds. Thread 5/6 Reputation assessment (independent scanners): • Scamadviser: 0/100 • Scam-Detector: 13.5/100 • Gridinsoft: 39/100 VirusTotal shows limited detections due to the domain’s young age and heavy protection, but mirrors are consistently linked to card fraud and phishing activity. Thread 6/6 De-anonymization result: Despite extensive pivoting across WHOIS history, forum threads, crypto flows, and Telegram artifacts, the owner remains fully anonymized. Professional OPSEC observed (privacy protection, fast-flux, disposable wallets, ticket-only contact). No real-name leaks identified. Recommendation: Immediate domain blocking across all mirrors. Continuous monitoring of crdpro.at and associated TG channels advised. Report to CERT-BH and Cloudflare Abuse. . Stay safe. #ThreatIntelligence #InfoSec

🚨A threat actor operating under the alias 'mosad' is offering classified US military documents for sale on underground forums, including FY-2027 posture statements from multiple US combatant commands. Activity on darkweb platforms reveals the actor is marketing sensitive defense materials through Telegram, Session, Tox, and Matrix channels.  The leaked documents include AFRICOM's FY-27 posture statement, CENTCOM's FY-27 posture statement, and combined Navy-Marine Corps FY-27 posture statements. Additional materials listed for sale include a Government Accountability Office report on Air Force depot maintenance and Air Force testimony covering bombers, tankers, and cargo aircraft. The actor claims access to 'TOP SECRET' reports and states they are 'not limited to USA' in their operations.  The threat actor has established a presence across multiple encrypted messaging platforms, operating Telegram channels including t.me/InsideMossad and t.me/dazer. They advertise sample documents and pricing information, with escrow services available for transactions. Previous activity attributed to 'mosad' has involved claims of exfiltrating classified documents from military research institutes and intelligence organizations, including alleged compromises of Chinese PLA facilities and DARPA-related technical reports.  US Africa Command and US Central Command posture statements are delivered annually to Congress through the Senate Armed Services Committee and represent critical assessments of US military strategy in their respective regions. AFRICOM covers US military operations across Africa, while CENTCOM oversees US military interests in the Middle East, Central Asia, and parts of South Asia. These documents typically contain sensitive operational planning, threat assessments, and resource allocation strategies.  The sale of such documents represents a significant national security concern, as posture statements inform congressional oversight of military spending and operational readiness. The actor's claims of access to classified defense materials across multiple US military branches suggest either a high-level insider threat or successful penetration of secure government networks. The US Department of Defense and relevant intelligence agencies would likely be investigating the source of the leak and the identity of the threat actor.

sample_tweets.text: my reaction when there's a supply chain attack

You must follow the Yellow Brick Road a/k/a be one of the following:  - Think like someone incredibly ignorant about computers - Be a high-profile target - Be a company - Be a crypto nerd  If you can successfully be one of these things, or larp as one, you will find the malware. Truthfully, I myself have a hard time finding malware in the wild. I am incapable of thinking like an internet doofus. I have tried many times.  I have noticed though that the easiest way to get free malware is looking for video game cheats. You can find tons upon tons of malware that targets Roblox nerds, CounterStrike nerds, and Call of Duty nerds.  There is this thing in the Roblox cheating community called an "Executor". Basically, it's a tool they utilize to perform process injection into the Roblox client. These "Executors" are NOTORIOUS for having impersonators which are actually malware and sometimes even the paid "Executors" betray their customer base. It is cool and badass if you like malware. It is not cool and not badass if you dislike malware.  Depending on what you're looking for though these fake Executors are almost always Information Stealing payloads, so you won't find much.  If you lookup aimbots for Call of Duty or Counter Strike you will almost always find malware on YouTube. They'll display a video of some dork spazzing out, blasting nerds through walls, and display the download link in the video description. To avoid detection the video description also has the "password" to the "aim bot". It's password protected to prevent automated scraping and automated detonation from anti-malware services.  There are other ways too, but this is the easiest and most low effort (in my opinion).  Okay, I got to go now. My wife says I have to go outside.

I have seen this with my own eyes, after my host institution offered a bonus for patent applications (not registrations!) my PhD-advisor promptly applied for a patent to convert garbage into energy with higher dimensional black hole relics (which, for all we know, don’t exist).    A research team with lead at Northwestern University in the United States reports a new type of academic fraud: fake patents. They have found multiple companies which sell credits on UK registered designs to academics, mostly in India, who can use them to boost promotion scores and institutional rankings.  UK registered designs are not technically patents. They protect appearance, not function, and are not checked for novelty before registration, which makes them fast and cheap to obtain. The companies sold these off for prices corresponding to roughly $20 to $400 per authorship. Many of the fake patents had long technical-sounding titles, multiple academic applicants, recycled images, and implausible products, including an alleged artificial intelligence skin-cancer device that appears to be a modified Glock pistol model (see image).

### 1. 현재 주요 사이버보안 트렌드
최근 공격자들은 AI 에이전트를 활용하여 취약점을 자동으로 탐색하고 공격 코드를 생성하는 등 공격의 자동화 및 고도화를 꾀하고 있습니다. 특히 데이터 암호화 중심의 랜섬웨어에서 데이터 유출 및 협박(Extortion) 위주의 캠페인으로 전략이 전환되고 있으며, 공급망 공격을 통한 침투 사례가 빈번하게 보고되고 있습니다. 또한, MITRE ATT&CK 프레임워크 기반의 은밀한 침투 기술이 주류를 이루며, 방어 측면에서는 AI 기반의 위협 탐지 및 대응 자동화가 필수적인 과제로 부상했습니다.

### 2. 주요 위협 행위자 / 캠페인
*   **Turla (BleepingComputer):** Kazuar backdoor를 모듈형 P2P botnet으로 진화시켜 지속적인 접근 및 데이터 수집 수행.
*   **UNC6671 (Mandiant):** BlackFile vishing extortion operation을 통해 기업 대상 금전 갈취 캠페인 전개.
*   **ShinyHunters (Krebs on Security):** Canvas 교육 플랫폼 해킹을 통해 대규모 데이터 유출 및 교육 기관 운영 중단 야기.
*   **Kimsuky (Securelist):** PebbleDash 기반 도구를 활용하여 AppleSeed malware cluster와 연계된 조직적 공격 수행.
*   **Keymous Plus (Twitter/X):** 모로코 공식 포털 대상 DDoS 공격 수행.

### 3. 한국 관련 위협
*   **의료 장비 제조사:** AhnLab ASEC 보고서에 따르면 대한민국 의료 초음파 장비 제조 기업의 데이터 유출 주장 확인.
*   **피싱 공격:** 이스트시큐리티(ESRC)는 국세청 및 하나은행을 사칭한 PhaaS(Phishing-as-a-Service) 기반의 기업 타겟형 피싱 메일 유포 확인.
*   **Pwn2Own Berlin 2026:** 한국 연구팀이 'Out-of-Bounds' 취약점 연구로 최종 3위 기록.

### 4. 주요 CVE / 취약점
*   **CVE-2026-42897:** Microsoft Exchange Server, Critical, 실제 악용 중.
*   **CVE-2026-20182:** Cisco Catalyst SD-WAN, Critical, 실제 악용 중.
*   **CVE-2026-0300:** PAN-OS Captive Portal, Critical, 실제 악용 중.

### 6. 즉각 대응 권고
*   **패치 적용:** Microsoft Exchange Server(CVE-2026-42897) 및 Cisco SD-WAN(CVE-2026-20182) 등 실제 악용 중인 취약점에 대해 즉각적인 보안 업데이트 수행.

*   **공급망 보안:** 오픈AI 사례와 같이 외부 라이브러리(TanStack 등) 및 오픈소스 확장 프로그램의 무결성 검증 프로세스 도입.
